mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are negative on average over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, the restrictive policy stance contracts forward earnings multiples and induces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate reached 4% in November 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales coincident with a declining unemployment rate indicates demand-pull expansion without slack erosion, amplifying profit margins and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns; neither series alone produces the signal.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS increases and UNRATE decreases jointly compared to random pairs (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating industrial production induces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because the Federal Reserve interprets supply strength as warranting additional rate hikes.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 3%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in > 55% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and negative wealth effects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1200 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index erodes real corporate earnings and triggers risk-premium re-rating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in > 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a high and rising federal funds rate triggers a joint discount-rate and earnings-recession repricing that produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 3%
failure_condition: Joint condition of rising UNRATE and FEDFUNDS > 3% fails to produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP contracting quarter-over-quarter signals an output gap deterioration that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 contractions coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year window.
